method_atom: a driven, out-of-equilibrium stochastic transport model in which many hard-core units hop unidirectionally along a one-dimensional discrete track under an excluded-volume constraint, where the units may occupy several consecutive sites and one or more localized bottlenecks with reduced transition rates throttle the flow; the steady-state throughput and occupancy profile depend sensitively on the placement of a single bottleneck and on the interference between two nearby bottlenecks, with limiting behavior as the bottleneck rate vanishes, benchmarked against a mean-field approximation.
object_atom: extended particles of size ℓ ≥ 1 (spanning multiple lattice spacings) performing totally asymmetric hopping on a one-dimensional lattice containing slow defect sites of hopping rate q < 1, motivated by biological molecular transport.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the biased single-file exclusion dynamics of extended hard-core units with localized rate defects and its current/density response to defect number and location; the domain-specific object is the size-ℓ particles on a defective lattice with its biological-transport significance.
